QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $708.93 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: Friday's session saw equities slide under pressure from surging Treasury yields and a 3% spike in WTI to $105.42 after Iran Hormuz ship seizures intensified. The Trump-Xi summit concluded without a semiconductor trade deal, sending the SOXX down 3.9% to 508.52. New Fed Chair Warsh faces a hawkish path with US10Y at 4.59% and inflation above target.
commodities_note: WTI jumped 3% to $105.42 on rising Hormuz tensions — the highest since the crisis began. Gold fell 1.4% to $4,555.80 as yields and the dollar strengthened. QQQ data anomaly: price $708.93 is outside the expected $250–$700 range, likely due to a contract rollover or unit mismatch; verify before using as a benchmark.

writeup.headline: WRD -3.9% below 50-day average — R:R 4.1:1, oversold, watch for bounce.
writeup.prior_period_delta_narrative: Close $7.23 → $7.14, RSI 41.8 → 40.5, extension -2.8% → -4.0% — nearing support at $6.85.
writeup.what_to_do: Bull mechanism: Robotaxi fleet expansion + L2++ ADAS commercialisation drives revenue over $150M in 2026. Bear mechanism: ADR delisting risk or slower-than-expected deployment in key markets. Headline R:R of 4.1:1 to $8.33 resistance is distorted by a 4% stop; use the 50-day at $7.43 as the mental invalidation. Entry zone $6.85–7.00, invalidation below $6.03. Fragility: ADR/China delisting risk — keep starter sizing.
writeup.thesis_break_condition: Thesis breaks if WRD fails to reclaim SMA50 ($7.43) on above-average volume within the next 10 sessions.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks remain CAUTION, with DBS and OCBC heavily overbought. UOB is marginally closer to its 50-day average but still blocked. NIM compression persists as US10Y stays above 4.20% and MAS S$NEER appreciation continues. No cluster-level divergence — it's a uniform wait for rate relief.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — awaiting NIM relief from US rates
singapore.key_development: US10Y at 4.59% keeps the dual headwind alive; no catalyst before October MAS review.

semiconductors.summary: The semiconductor cluster is uniformly CAUTION after a 3.9% SOXX decline to 508.52. All names are extended above their 50-day averages, with many in extreme territory (>20%). The Trump-Xi summit delivered no chip deal, dashing hopes of an H200 export breakthrough. Samsung's May 21 strike risk adds near-term supply uncertainty, but the structural AI capex thesis remains intact. Divergence is absent — it's a sector-wide overextension that requires a pullback.
semiconductors.key_development: Post-summit sell-off wipes out recent gains; semiconductor divergence remains muted. Samsung strike threatens memory supply.

big_tech.summary: BigTech remains blocked with all four names on CAUTION. MSFT is the closest to a watch-list upgrade, sitting just 5.7% above its 50-day and with a reasonable 21.8x forward PE, but the tight upside at $425.35 needs to be cleared. AMZN and GOOG are heavily extended. PLTR is below its 50-day but is capped by a 65x PE. The cluster lacks a near-term catalyst to break out of the technical logjam.
big_tech.thesis_status: Structurally mixed — capex confirmed, but credit-circularity risk remains
big_tech.key_development: Ackman's MSFT stake is a positive signal, but Gates Foundation's exit introduces offsetting noise. Google I/O on May 20-21 is the next potential catalyst.

active_risks: Iran Hormuz blockade: Ship seizures intensified, WTI surged to $105.42; Trump rejected Iran's proposal, no ceasefire in sight.
US-China trade summit failure: No chip deal reached; export controls on H200 still in place, China prioritizing domestic chips.
Fed/Warsh hawkish policy: US10Y 4.59%, inflation remains above 2%; new Fed Chair Warsh faces challenging rate path.
Samsung labor strike risk: 45,000 workers to strike May 21, threatening HBM memory supply to NVDA and Tesla.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil above $100 is raising input costs across sectors and pressuring consumer spending. Semis sold off on trade deal disappointment, with SOXX down 3.9%. Hawkish Fed posture keeps tech multiples compressed, and the Samsung strike adds a supply-chain wildcard for NVDA and TSM.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump-Xi summit concluded with no semiconductor trade breakthrough; US trade rep Greer confirmed chip controls were not a major topic. Samsung confirmed a strike starting May 21 despite talks.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash; no new entries until at least one of the three overhangs (Hormuz escalation, trade deal, or Fed pivot) materially improves.
